summary: The FY24 peak (56.8%) followed operational changes DSNY made to truck routing that year. The subsequent decline is tied to the citywide rollout of curbside composting: many collection trucks now carry two separate bins and must offload at two different facilities, often far apart, which takes more time and leaves less room in a shift to complete a second trip to the dump.

sources.label: DSNY, Preliminary Fiscal 2026 Mayor's Management Report

oneLiner: increasing overall, though composting's two-bin trucks have slowed recent shifts
